summary: Named several previously anonymous game-mode struct fields and propagated those names through 1P/adventure/game-over code, while tightening related function prototypes and small type choices. The cleanup directly enabled 3 new matches in gm_17C0: gm_8017CA38, gm_8017CBAC, and gm_80180AF4, for +896 matched bytes according to the decomp.dev bot report.
what_changed: Renamed Unk1PData fields in src/melee/gm/types.h from offset-style names to semantic names: x0 -> ckind, x1 -> color, x2 -> cpu_level, x3 -> slot, and x5 -> stocks.
Renamed nested Unk1PData_x24.x0 to ckind and updated call sites that iterate over companion/follow-up character data.
Named DebugGameOverData fields at offsets 0x10 and 0x14 as ckind and slot, and added explicit offset comments for that struct.
Changed gmm_x0_528_t.stocks from s8 to u8 and updated gm_801B0730's stocks pointer parameter from s8* to u8*.
Annotated gmm_x1F2C bitfields b789, b10_to_12, and b13_to_15 as stocks-related fields.
Replaced magic/anonymous field accesses throughout gm_17C0.c and gmadventure.c with ckind, color, cpu_level, slot, and stocks names.
Converted several uses of raw character constants to named constants such as CKIND_KIRBY, CKIND_ZELDA, CKIND_SEAK, and CHKIND_NONE where shown in the diff.

Updated prototypes to better express parameter meaning, including ftBossLib_8015C530(int cpu_level), fn_80162BFC(s8 ckind, int), fn_80162DF8(s8 ckind, int), fn_80162FF4(s8 ckind, int), gm_80164024(u8 ckind), and gm_801BEFA4(s8 ckind).
Adjusted gm_16F1 prototypes and definition widths for functions such as fn_80173098, gm_80173224, and gm_8017390C from u8 arguments to int arguments where needed.
Replaced a local anonymous IntroData struct in gmadventure.c with the named IntroData type.
Implemented previously stubbed/missing matches in gm_17C0.c: gm_8017CA38, gm_8017CBAC, and gm_80180AF4.
Updated gm_8017E430's header comment to document that it returns a player slot, and changed gm_80180AF4's declaration to return Fighter_GObj* instead of UNK_RET.
smart_moves: The PR used small semantic field names to unlock larger matches: once ckind, cpu_level, slot, color, and stocks were named consistently, decompiled gm_17C0 control flow became readable enough to land two larger functions.
It propagated type/name changes through headers, source files, and Doxygen declarations together, avoiding mismatched prototypes across compilation units.
It replaced sentinel and character magic values with established enum-style constants where possible, making comparisons such as CHKIND_NONE and CKIND_KIRBY self-documenting.
It corrected signedness/width around stocks from s8 to u8 in the shared struct and CSS helper signature, matching observed usage without changing layout size.
It used real gameplay semantics from call sites: audio loading, CSS transfer, adventure save/progress, game-over handling, and player slot return paths all reinforced the same field meanings.
decomp_lessons: Field naming can be a matching tactic, not just documentation: semantic names reduce ambiguity enough to safely decompile adjacent functions.
When a struct field is inferred, update all direct users in the same PR so future decomp work sees a coherent data model instead of mixed xN and semantic names.
Stock counters in these gm structs are treated as unsigned byte values in at least part of the flow; avoid assuming s8 just because nearby character kinds are s8.
Character kind fields in this area use names like ckind/c_kind and are often compared with CKIND_* or CHKIND_* constants; replacing raw numeric checks is useful evidence.
Header prototype cleanup should accompany source cleanup, especially for unknown gm functions whose parameters are becoming semantically identified.
A small leaf function can sometimes be matched once its true return type is known: gm_80180AF4 became a simple Fighter_GObj* returning Player_GetEntity(1).
naming_conventions: Use ckind for CharacterKind/character-kind fields in Unk1PData-style structs.
Existing gmm_x0_528_t uses c_kind with an underscore; this PR preserves that style there rather than forcing ckind everywhere.
Use color, cpu_level, slot, and stocks for the common 1P/adventure player selection fields.
Use CHKIND_NONE for no character/child-character sentinel values instead of raw 0x21 where applicable.
Use CKIND_* constants such as CKIND_KIRBY, CKIND_ZELDA, and CKIND_SEAK instead of raw character IDs when evidence is clear.
Add parameter names in prototypes when only the parameter meaning is known, e.g. s8 ckind or int cpu_level, without prematurely naming the function itself.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Three functions newly matched in main/melee/gm/gm_17C0: gm_8017CA38 (+372 bytes), gm_8017CBAC (+488 bytes), and gm_80180AF4 (+36 bytes).
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code improved to 40.75%, +0.02%, +896 bytes.
Prototype widening from u8 to int for some gm_16F1 functions suggests matching required argument types that reflect ABI/register behavior rather than only the logical value range.
Correcting gm_801B0730's stocks pointer to u8* and gmm_x0_528_t.stocks to u8 likely helped align loads/stores and avoid signedness artifacts.
Using named struct fields exposed repeated save/progress patterns in gm_8017CBAC: update per-character progress, compare cpu_level, and record best stocks for cases 21, 22, and 23.
gm_80180AF4 matched as a direct return of Player_GetEntity(1) once its return type was declared as Fighter_GObj*.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only recorded feedback was the decomp.dev bot report confirming 3 new matches and +896 matched bytes.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining Unk1PData x4/x6/x7/x8/xC fields and infer names from gm_17C0 and gmadventure call sites.
Search for raw 0x21 character sentinel comparisons in gm code that can be converted to CHKIND_NONE.
Audit remaining raw character ID comparisons such as 0x12/0x13 for CKIND_ZELDA and CKIND_SEAK opportunities.
Review gm_16F1 functions with widened int parameters to see whether more prototypes can be named or matched.
Trace gmm_x1F2C stock bitfields b789, b10_to_12, and b13_to_15 to determine precise player/index meanings.
